searchLedger.hypothesis: createMemoryRecord validation, identity, dedup, lineage, and return path preserved

searchActions.result: Current createMemoryRecord still resolves record identity at lines 289-293, finds same-path existing rows at lines 312-319, retires predecessors before insert at lines 324-328, inserts exactly once through vectorIndex at lines 330-364, applies post-insert metadata at lines 375-390, records lineage/history at lines 405-448, and returns indexWithMetadata() at lines 450-453. The removed local source-kind helpers are present in write-provenance.ts lines 31-94 and persistence is centralized at lines 155-164.

searchLedger.rationale: The -107 reduction moved source-kind/provenance helpers out of create-record and replaced persistSourceKind with applyWriteProvenance after memory_id exists. I did not find removed create-record identity, validation, dedup, lineage, or error-handling logic.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Only provenance/source-kind persistence was centralized; core create-record behavior remains present.

searchLedger.hypothesis: memory_update constitutional/manual guard still protects automated edits

searchActions.result: buildGuardedUpdateParams derives sourceKind at lines 98-103, treats non-human as automated at lines 104-106, skips protected fields title/triggerPhrases/importanceTier at lines 112-123, and keeps protected rows' storedSourceKind at lines 130-132. The transaction persists source kind and provenance at lines 321-325, and the response exposes skippedFields at lines 516-523.

searchLedger.rationale: The -26 net refactor removed the local hasSourceKindColumn/persistUpdateSourceKind helper, but did not remove the constitutional/manual guard branches.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Guard logic remains intact and still skips protected automated field updates.

searchActions.result: processPreparedMemory still returns checkExistingRow before embedding/write at lines 2303-2314 and content-hash duplicate results at lines 2388-2399 and 2541-2555. dedup.ts returns status:'unchanged' at lines 291-301 and status:'duplicate' at lines 353-374. New writes still build status indexed/deferred/updated in response-builder.ts lines 319-326.

searchLedger.rationale: The provenance addition is not reached for direct duplicate/unchanged early returns, and new/update result shaping still depends on existing plus embeddingStatus as before.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No status or response-shape regression found for direct memory_save.

title: Default provenance turns ungoverned scan/ingest calls into governed requests
evidence: memory-index.ts now calls validateGovernedIngest({ ...args, provenanceSource: args.provenanceSource ?? 'memory_index_scan', provenanceActor: args.provenanceActor ?? 'system-scan' }) at lines 356-360; memory-ingest.ts does the same with memory_ingest_start/async-ingest at lines 146-150. scope-governance.ts:235-242 treats any provenanceSource/provenanceActor string as requiresGovernedIngest(), then lines 286-290 require tenantId, sessionId, and userId or agentId.
rationale: Previously validateGovernedIngest(args) allowed ungoverned scan/ingest calls when no scope/provenance/retention fields were supplied. Injecting default provenance before validation makes ordinary calls such as memory_index_scan({specFolder}) or memory_ingest_start({paths}) fail with missing governed-scope fields. That is a behavior change beyond tagging.

title: Existing chunk parent provenance is updated before safe-swap success is known
evidence: For existing chunked parents, lines 188-193 call applyWriteProvenance(database, pid, writeProvenance) and return from setupParent before child indexing and safe-swap finalization. Later failure paths at lines 475-485 and 563-596 can return status:'error' while the retained parent row has already had provenance updated.
rationale: This does not duplicate the memory row, but it can tag a retained existing parent as written by the attempted operation even when the re-chunk write fails. If provenance is intended to describe successful persisted writes, it should be applied after finalizeSwapTx succeeds or rolled back with the failed attempt.
